info.title: NextGen Catalog API
info.description:
This is the NextGen Catalog API application made with Express and documented with Swagger.

This API requires the use of an API key in order to access. Once you have obtained an API key, you can pass the API key into a REST API call in the x-api-key header of the request. For example, the request might look like

    curl --location --request GET 'https://catalog.archives.gov/api/v2/records/search?q=constitution'
    --header 'Content-Type: application/json'
    --header 'x-api-key: API_KEY'

where API_KEY is the key string of your API key.

For write operations, in addition to the API key, a user ID in the format of a Universally Unique Identifier (UUID) is required in the body of the request. For example, a request to POST a tag might look like:

    curl --location --request POST 'https://catalog.archives.gov/api/v2/tags'
    --header 'Content-Type: application/json'
    --data-raw '{
    "tag": "example tag",
    "targetNaId": 1667751,
    "userId": "USER_UUID"
    }'
where USER_UUID is the UUID of the user.

Please contact O&M at Catalog_API@nara.gov for an API Key.

get.summary: Recieve the users notifications.
get.description: Recieve the users notifications, including edited transcriptions, moderated contributions, and replies to comments. Notifications are contributions, with an additional field 'notificationType' to denote type.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: userId of the user recieving notifications.

parameters.name: countOnly
parameters.description: Recieve only the count of notifications, and none of the contributions.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: Recieve only the notifications past a certain date. Date must be in YYYY-MM-DD format.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Limit the amount of returned notifications.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number for pagination of notifications. Page size is denoted by limit, defaults to 5. For example, if page = 3 notifications 10-14 will be returned.

200.description: Successfully retrieved notifications

abolishDate.title: abolishDate
abolishDate.description: The date on which the organization was terminated, disbanded, inactivated, or superseded.

accessRestriction.title: accessRestriction
accessRestriction.description: The indication of, explicit markings for, and detailed notes regarding access restrictions on the archival materials.  Alerts users if access to the archival materials may be restricted.

note.description: Significant information pertaining to the access restrictions on archival materials.
examples: The records may be restricted due to privacy concerns. Register numbers 1 through 900 have been screened and are open for research.

status.description: The indication of whether or not there are access restrictions on the archival materials.

accessionNumber.title: accessionNumber
accessionNumber.description: The unique identifier assigned to a group of archival materials that have been transferred into the legal custody of NARA.  Links the description of the archival materials and their acquisition/accession documentation.

administrativeHistoryNote.title: administrativeHistoryNote
administrativeHistoryNote.description: Explanations or significant information regarding the organization, including information relevant to an understanding of its creation, mission, functions, program areas, activities, incumbents, administrative and operational hierarchy, relationships to other corporate bodies, relationships with superior organizations, and earlier or successor names. Establishes an appropriate context for understanding the records created by an organization.
examples: MACV was abolished March 29, 1973, by authority of JCS message 4825 DTG 220021Z Nov 72.

Following U.S. entry into the war the Special Operations Group (SPOBS) became an advance element of a theater of operations and was redesignated Headquarters, U.S. Army Forces in the British Isles by direction of the President conveyed in War Department message 293, AGWAR to SPOBS, January 8, 1942, and announced by HQ USAFBI General Order 1, January 8, 1942.

ancestorCollection.title: ancestorCollection
ancestorCollection.description: Identifiable and pertinent information that represents a collection that is a parent or more distant ancestor to another archival description or control group.

ancestorDescription.title: ancestorDescription
ancestorDescription.description: Base model for ancestors (parent, grandparent, etc..) to descriptions

ancestorFileUnit.title: ancestorFileUnit
ancestorFileUnit.description: Identifiable and pertinent information that represents a file unit that is a parent or more distant ancestor of another archival description.

ancestorRecordGroup.title: ancestorRecordGroup
ancestorRecordGroup.description: Identifiable and pertinent information that repsents a record group that is a parent or more distant ancestor to another archival description or control group.

ancestorSeries.title: ancestorSeries
ancestorSeries.description: Identifiable and pertinent information that represents a series that is a parent or more distant ancestor of another archival description.

arrangement.title: arrangement
arrangement.description: The pattern or ordering sequence, such as alphabetical or chronological, of the archival material.  Helps users locate particular archival materials within a series or file unit.

audiovisual.title: audiovisual
audiovisual.description: Indicates if a file unit or series is audio-visual in nature.

authorityReference.title: authorityReference
authorityReference.description: Heading and identifier of a primary authority record.  Represents a relationship between authorities or between authorities and descriptions.

authorityType.title: authorityType
authorityType.description: Type of the primary authority used to control descriptive and entitic terms on archival materials.

beginCongress.title: beginCongress
beginCongress.description: The beginning number of the Congress during which the archival materials were created.

biographicalNote.title: biographicalNote
biographicalNote.description: Explanations or significant information relevant to the understanding of a person's life or activities.
examples: Sarah Weddington was born in Abilene Texas in 1945. In 1965 she received her B.S. degree from McMurry College in Abilene, and in 1967 her J.D. from the University of Texas, Austin. She was a Texas state legislator and in private practice in Austin from 1972 to 1977, when she came to Washington to be the General Counsel of the Department of Agriculture. In October 1978 she became Special Assistant (Assistant after September 1979) to the President for Women's Affairs. In 1981 she returned to Austin to practice law.

birthDate.title: birthDate
birthDate.description: The date on which the person was born.

broadcastDate.title: broadcastDate
broadcastDate.description: The date on which the item was first broadcast or another known broadcast date, if first date is unknown.

broaderTerm.title: broaderTerm
broaderTerm.description: An authorized, preferred heading that is broader in definition and scope

collectionIdentifier.title: collectionIdentifier
collectionIdentifier.description: The unique identifier assigned to a collection.

contextualAuthorityType.title: contextualAuthorityType
contextualAuthorityType.description: The type of primary authority used to provide context by capturing the historical background of the organizations and individuals who created, contributed to, or are the subject of archival materials.

contribution.title: contribution
contribution.description: The text of the tag, comment, or transcription entered by a user.
examples: Four score and seven years ago...

contributionId.title: contributionId
contributionId.description: Uniquely identifies a public contribution among other contributions of the same type.

contributionSequence.title: contributionSequence
contributionSequence.description: Represents the order in which contributions are made in relation to a parent contribution.

contributionTarget.title: contributionTarget
contributionTarget.description: Values needed to identify the description, authority, or digital object that is the target of this contribution.

contributionType.title: contributionType
contributionType.description: Identifies the type of public contribution.

contributor.title: contributor
contributor.description: An organization or person, other than the archival creator, responsible for the intellectual, technical, artistic, or financial production of the archival materials.

contributorType.title: contributorType
contributorType.description: The role of an individual who or an organization that contributed or contributes to archival materials.

coordinates.title: coordinates
coordinates.description: Indicates the position of the place, expressed in degrees/minutes/seconds in accor. Latitude is the angular distance north or south of the equator, measured along a meridian. Longitude is the angular distance east or west of the Prime Meridian, which runs through Greenwich, England. Meridians are imaginary north-south lines on the Earth's surface that connect both geographic poles, used to measure longitude.

copyrightDate.title: copyrightDate
copyrightDate.description: The date on which the item was copyrighted. (A copyright date does not mean an item is still copyrighted.)

coverageEndDate.title: coverageEndDate
coverageEndDate.description: The end of the time period covered by the subject(s) of the archival materials.

coverageStartDate.title: coverageStartDate
coverageStartDate.description: The beginning of the time period covered by the subject(s) of the archival materials.

createdAt.title: createdAt
createdAt.description: Date and time the public contribution was created.

creatingIndividual.title: creatingIndividual
creatingIndividual.description: A person responsible for the creation, accumulation, or maintenance of the series when in working (primary) use.

creatingOrganization.title: creatingOrganization
creatingOrganization.description: The name of an organization responsible for the creation, accumulation, or maintenance of the series when in working (primary) use.

creator.title: creator
creator.description: The name of a person or organization responsible for the creation, accumulation, or maintenance of the series when in working (primary) use.

creatorType.title: creatorType
creatorType.description: The means for indicating if a person or organization is the most recent or predecessor creator of archival materials.

custodialHistoryNote.title: custodialHistoryNote
custodialHistoryNote.description: The description of the custodial history for the archival materials from the time of their creation to the time of their accessioning by NARA. This information may be particularly important for personal papers, donated materials, and Federal records that do not come to NARA through the regular government records transfer process. Provides information on any changes of ownership or breaks in the government chain-of-custody that may impact the interpretation of the archival materials or are significant for ensuring their authenticity and integrity.
examples: In 1983 the U.S. Air Force loaned its pre-1945 and Korean War-era photographs and related documentation (including the subject index) to the National Air and Space Museum, Smithsonian Institution, in accordance with a Memorandum of Agreement. The purpose of the loan was, in part, to enable the National Air and Space Museum to produce a videodisc of the collection. The records remained at the museum until 1998 when they were accessioned into the National Archives.

The nucleus of the Stephen T. Mather Collection was placed in the Prints and Photographs Division of the Library of Congress in 1945. In 1954 it was transferred back to the National Park Service (NPS). While the collection was housed at the Library of Congress, the NPS added to it on a regular basis. No additions to the collection were made after 1954. The National Archives accessioned the Mather Collection in 1963.

dataControlGroup.title: dataControlGroup
dataControlGroup.description: Defines which office within NARA owns or is the custodian of the archival record.

groupCode.description: A list of organizational units that describe a data control group.

groupName.description: The name of the data control group.
examples: National Archives at Washington, DC - Textual Reference

dateNote.title: dateNote
dateNote.description: The explanation of various dates or predominant bulk dates or gaps in the archival materials.
examples: The bulk of these are from the period from 1940 to mid-1960s.

deathDate.title: deathDate
deathDate.description: The date on which the person died.

descriptiveAuthorityType.title: descriptiveAuthorityType
descriptiveAuthorityType.description: The type of primary authority used to control specific terms that help describe archival materials.

digitalObject.title: digitalObject
digitalObject.description: The digital representation of all or part of the archival materials being described.

extractedText.description: Text that was able to be extracted from the digital object.
examples: Four score and seven years ago our fathers brought forth...
We the People of the United States, in Order to form a more perfect Union...

otherExtractedText.description: Text that was contributed by the NARA partners.
examples: Some text extracted by another OCR technology
Some text extracted by another advanced technology

extractedTextAccurracy.description: A percentage representing how accurately the text extracted from the digital object matches the text displayed in the object.

objectDescription.description: A brief description of the digital object.
examples: Only the first page of this 12-page letter is available online.

objectDesignator.description: An identifier for each digital object when there is more than one digital object associated with an archival description.

objectFileSize.description: The size in bytes of the computer file containing the access version of the digital object.

objectFilename.description: The porition of the object URL that identifies the file without any directory or location.

objectType.description: The type or file format of the digital object.

objectUrl.description: The identifier assigned to the file containing the access version of the digital object.

thumbnailUrl.description: The identifier assigned to the file containing the thumbnail version of the digital object.

dispositionAuthorityNumber.title: dispositionAuthorityNumber
dispositionAuthorityNumber.description: The unique identifier assigned by NARA to a category of records indicating that their disposition has been approved by NARA.  This is also known as the schedule number.

distance.title: distance
distance.description: Indicates number of nodes removed an ancestor is from a descendent description.

donor.title: donor
donor.description: The name of an organization or person that has donated a collection of archival materials.

editStatus.title: editStatus
editStatus.description: The identification of the production or pre-production stage of audiovisual materials (motion pictures, sound recordings or video recordings).

endCongress.title: endCongress
endCongress.description: The last number of the Congress during which the archival materials were created.

establishDate.title: establishDate
establishDate.description: The date on which the organization was established.

fileUnitCount.title: fileUnitCount
fileUnitCount.description: Total count of file units which are children of this object.

fileUnitPhysicalOccurrence.title: fileUnitPhysicalOccurrence
fileUnitPhysicalOccurrence.description: Details about the physical occurrence of archival materials within a file unit.

findingAid.title: findingAid
findingAid.description: Tools that help users more easily find archival descriptions and materials.

fileFormat.description: The type or file format of the online finding aid.

note.description: The explanation of significant information about a finding aid.
examples: An alphabetical list of cities can be found in the research room.

source.description: The author or creator of the finding aid.

url.description: The URL associated with a finding aid.

urlNote.description: Explanation of significant information about a finding aid url.
examples: This Online Resource URL is a finding aid for naturalizations.

urlDescription.description: Description of the information contained at a finding aid url
examples: This Online Resource URL is a finding aid for naturalizations.

formerCollection.title: formerCollection
formerCollection.description: The identifiable information of the collection to which the archival materials were previously allocated. Enables users to locate archival materials by their former collection citations, which can be found in out-of-date finding aids.

formerRecordGroup.title: formerRecordGroup
formerRecordGroup.description: The number and indentifiable information of the record group to which the archival materials were previously allocated. Enables users to locate archival materials by their former record group citations, which can be found in out-of-date finding aids, such as earlier versions of the Guide to Federal Records in the National Archives of the United States.formerRecordGroup model description

formerlyContained.title: formerlyContained
formerlyContained.description: A list of naId values that were previously a part of the archival control group.

formerlyContainedBy.title: formerlyContainedBy
formerlyContainedBy.description: A list of naId values representing archival control groups of which this series was a part.

fullerFormOfName.title: fullerFormOfName
fullerFormOfName.description: A more complete form of a person's name than is indicated in the name field.

functionAndUse.title: functionAndUse
functionAndUse.description: The description of why the archival materials were created. This element differs from scopeAndContentNote which describes the significant information contained within the records. This data element is about the activities that resulted in the creation of the archival materials. The scopeAndContentNote value is what is in the archival materials.
examples: The Reports of Death were created at field hospitals or by battlefield commanders and were later used by Sextons to complete Internment Records.
The minute books were created to record the court's activities on a daily basis, including both criminal and civil litigation.

generalMediaType.title: generalMediaType
generalMediaType.description: The general physical media form of the archival materials.

generalNote.title: generalNote
generalNote.description: Significant information that does not belong in any other element. Provides a place to capture information significant to the public that is not appropriate for any other element.
examples: This series serves as an index to the series 'Correspondence of the Director, 1880-1932.'
This series was known as 'Miscellaneous' in previous finding aids.

generalRecordType.title: generalRecordType
generalRecordType.description: The general intellectual form of the archival materials. Allows users to search for archival materials by general records types.

geographicPlaceNameLinkCounts.title: geographicPlaceNameLinkCounts
geographicPlaceNameLinkCounts.description: Tracks the number and manner of the relationships to a geographic place name authority record.

geographicPlaceName.description: Total numer of times this geographic place name is referred to in another geographic place name authority record.

jurisdiction.description: Total number of times this geographic place name acts as the juristiction of an organization.

organization.description: Total number of times this geographic place anem is referred to in an organization authority record.

subject.description: Total number of times this geographic place name acts as the subject of archival materials.

totalDescription.description: Total number of times this geographic place name is referred to, in any way, in archival materials.

geographicReference.title: geographicReference
geographicReference.description: The geographic area represented in the archival materials.

heading.title: heading
heading.description: A version of the authorized name of an authority that includes additional information such as dates or fuller forms of names.
examples: Department of Defense. Department of the Navy. U.S. Naval Support Activity, Chu Lai, Vietnam. 1964-1971

holdingsMeasurement.title: holdingsMeasurement
holdingsMeasurement.description: The unit and quantity of the archival materials in the series.

count.description: The quantity of the archival materials in the series. For artifacts, it counts the number of artifacts in the series. For electronic data, it counts the number of logical data records in the series. For all other records, it counts the number of each type of physical container in which archival materials are stored.

type.description: The unit by which archival materials are counted physically for performance measures. The unit is either a physical container or physical entity. Examples of physical containers include legal-size or letter-size boxes, aerial film cans, cabinet drawers, and bound volumes. Physical entities include artifacts and logical data records.

importRecordControlNumber.title: importRecordControlNumber
importRecordControlNumber.description: The number assigned by the organization that created or distributed an imported authority record. It provides traceability for an imported authority record back to the original external source.

inclusiveEndDate.title: inclusiveEndDate
inclusiveEndDate.description: The ending date on which the record group, collection, series, or item was created, maintained, or accumulated by the creator.

inclusiveStartDate.title: inclusiveStartDate
inclusiveStartDate.description: The beginning date on which the record group, collection, series, or item was created, maintained, or accumulated by the creator.

internalTransferNumber.title: internalTransferNumber
internalTransferNumber.description: The unique identifier assigned to a group of archival materials that was transferred within NARA. This type of information is particularly important for archival materials that have been regionalized.

itemAvMediaOccurrence.title: itemAvMediaOccurrence
itemAvMediaOccurrence.description: Additional media occurrence information available for audtiovisual items.

base.description: The substance of the media used to physically carry or hold the information in the archival item. Also called the substrate.

elementNumber.description: The number assigned to an individual physical component that makes up a reel, tape, or disc. Commonly used when a film reel is made up of separate sound and picture elements, or when a film reel is composed of A and B rolls. For example, a film reel may be composed of an image reel and a soundtrack reel. The image reel could be designated element number 1, and the soundtrack reel could be designated element number 2.

emulsion.description: The type of coating that is bonded to and supported by the base of the specific media type.

footage.description: The length in feet for an individual roll or reel in an audiovisual item.

format.description: The format of the media comprising the audiovisual materials. For example, the format of videotape can be VHS or Betamax, the format for sound recordings can be Master or Stamper Matrix, or the format for motion pictures can be Duplicate Negative Composite or Master Projection Print Composite.

recordingSpeed.description: The speed at which audio recordings are recorded or reproduced. For audio tapes, the measurement is inches per second (ips). For audio discs or cylinders, the measurement is revolutions per minute (rpm).

reelTapeDiscNumber.description: Could not find description in LCDRG

roll.description: Indication of the roll used in a motion picture. In creating a film, alternate scenes are typically assembled in checkerboard fashion on two or more different rolls with black leaders between scenes to facilitate fades and dissolves and to ultimately produce a single strip master positive from which a duplicating negative (dupe neg) can then be made.

runningTime.description: The duration in seconds of the individual parts (reels, tapes, rolls) of an audiovisual item.

soundtrackConfiguration.description: The recording configuration of a sound recording or motion picture soundtrack.

soundtrackLanguage.description: The language of the soundtrack for a motion picture or video recording.

tapeThickness.description: The thickness of the tape on which the audiovisual item has been recorded.

wind.description: The playback direction of the reel/roll.

totalFootage.description: The length in feet of all the reels or rolls that make up the entire copy of an audiovisual item.

totalRunningTime.description: The total duration of an audiovisual item in seconds.

itemCommon.title: itemCommon
itemCommon.description: The smallest indivisible archival unit (e.g. a letter, memorandum, report, leaflet, or photograph). For example, a book or record album would be described as an item, but the individual chapters of the book or the discs or songs that make up the album would not be described as items.

ancestors.description: Identifiable and otherwise relevant information about archival control groups or hierarchical description levels that are parents or more distant ancestors to this file unit.

itemCount.title: itemCount
itemCount.description: Number of items (including audiovisual items) within this series or file unit

itemMediaOccurrence.title: itemMediaOccurrence
itemMediaOccurrence.description: Additional media occurrence information only available for items.

base.description: The substance of the media used to physically carry or hold the information in the archival item. Also called the substrate.

emulsion.description: The type of coating that is bonded to and supported by the base of the specific media type.

jurisdiction.title: jurisdiction
jurisdiction.description: The geographic area over which the organization had an administrative responsibility or about which it collected information.  Represents a geographical name authority record.

language.title: language
language.description: The language used in the archival materials.

levelOfDescription.title: levelOfDescription
levelOfDescription.description: The archival control group level (record group or collection) or hierarchical description level (series, file unit, or item) of the archival materials.

linkCounts.title: linkCounts
linkCounts.description: Tracks the number and manner of the relationships between records.

totalDescription.description: Total number of times this person is referred to, in any way, in archival materials.

localIdentifier.title: localIdentifier
localIdentifier.description: The identifier that a NARA custodial unit specifies to be used to request archival materials in the unit's custody.

location.title: location
location.description: Information about where the archival materials are kept.

facility.description: The building where archival materials are kept.
examples: National Archives Building - Archives I (Washington, DC)

note.description: Location Note indicates the particular location of the archival materials within a building.
examples: These materials are located in Research Room 5050.
These items are located in the cold vault.

mediaOccurrence.title: mediaOccurrence
mediaOccurrence.description: Provides information about the physical description of the archival materials and additional information used to describe the media occurrence of the archival materials.

color.description: The absence or presence of colors, besides black, white and gray, of the specific media type.

containerId.description: The identifier or number for the individual container storing each specific media type.

depth.description: The distance measured from front to back of a specific media type.

dimension.description: The standard size of the physical items of a specific media type.

height.description: The distance measured from top to bottom of the specific media type.

mediaOccurrenceNote.description: Explanations or significant information relating to the media occurrence of the archival materials.
examples: This reel contains music and sound effects.
These images were made by the albumen and collodion processes.

physicalRestrictionNote.description: Information regarding any physical restrictions that apply to the particular specific media type of the archival materials. Physical restrictions are primarily preservation restrictions.
examples: Because these records are stored offsite, researchers must request the records 48 hours in advance.
This film reel has shrunk and may not be viewed.

pieceCount.description: The exact number of physical items comprising the specific media type of the archival materials.

process.description: The technical or manual means by which the specific media type was created.

reproductionCount.description: The exact number of parts or sides of the physical items needed to create a complete duplicate of the specific media type of the archival materials.

specificMediaType.description: The physical form of the archival materials.

technicalAccessRequirementsNote.description: A description of the equipment needed to view the specific media type, such as a light table to view aerial film, or hardware and software to view archival materials on electronic media.
examples: An open reel computer tape drive is required.
A light table is needed to view these aerial photographs.

width.description: The horizontal distance measured from side to side of a specific media type.

microformPublication.title: microformPublication
microformPublication.description: Information about the microform publication of the archival material. Microform publications are reproductions of archival materials, which are made available in research rooms for consultation and sometimes marketed and sold.

identifier.description: The unique identifier associated with a microform publication.

note.description: Explanations or significant information regarding a microform publication in relation to the archival materials being described.
examples: These records have been reproduced in full in this microform publication.
Records in this series from 1917-1920 are available in this microform publication.

title.description: The unique title associated with a microform publication.
examples: Dunn Rolls of 1867 and 1869, Citizens and Freedmen of the Creek Nation, 1867-1869
Captured North Vietnamese Documents of the Combined Document Exploitation Center, 1950-1975

name.title: name
name.description: The authorized form of an authority record name.

narrowerTerm.title: narrowerTerm
narrowerTerm.description: An authorized, preferred heading that is narrower in definition and scope.

numberingNote.title: numberingNote
numberingNote.description: The explanation of an agency or NARA-assigned numbering scheme.
examples: Requests for case files in this series must include the final certificate number, which is often found in tract books or on patent documents. When requesting canceled or relinquished case files under the Homestead Act and Timber Culture Acts, include the application number.

numerator.title: numerator
numerator.description: A roman numeral which is the subsequent part of a person's forename (first name). In general, a numerator is usually only part of the names of popes and royalty.

objectId.title: objectId
objectId.description: The identifier given to a digital object.

onlineResource.title: onlineResource
onlineResource.description: A digital resource, such as a web page or PDF file, that resides outside of the DAS database and enhances understanding of and access to archival materials described within it. Online Resource contains information about the archival materials or their use that is not part of a standard archival description. Online Resource may be created by NARA or by external sources, and may be mounted on NARA or external web sites.

description.description: Textual description of the type of online resource.

note.description: Explanations or significant information regarding an Online Resource in relation to the archival materials being described.
examples: The electronic records in some of the files in this series can be searched online via the Access to Archival Databases (AAD) system.
Order Online! can be used to order copies of these records.

url.description: The URL associated with an Online Resource.

orgLinkCounts.title: orgLinkCounts
orgLinkCounts.description: Tracks the number and manner of relationships between organizations and other archival materials.

contributor.description: Total number of times this organization acts as a contributor to archival materials.

creator.description: Total number of times this organiation acts as a creator for archival materials.

donor.description: Total number of times this object acts as a donor for archival material.

subject.description: Total number of times this organization acts as a subject to archival materials.

totalDescription.description: Total number of times this organization is referred to in an approved archival description.

organizationName.title: organizationName
organizationName.description: Represents the name of the organization being described in the authority record. The official designation of an organization

organizationalContributor.title: organizationalContributor
organizationalContributor.description: The name of an organization, other than the archival creator, responsible for the intellectual, technical, artistic, or financial production of the archival materials.

organizationalDonor.title: organizationalDonor
organizationalDonor.description: The name of an organization that has donated a collection of archival materials. Organizational entities include meetings, conferences, commissions, production groups, and Federal agencies. They can be any entity other than an individual.

organizationalReference.title: organizationalReference
organizationalReference.description: The name of an organization that is the subject of or highly relevant to the archival materials.

otherPreservationCharacteristics.title: otherPreservationCharacteristics
otherPreservationCharacteristics.description: Additional information about the physical nature of the archival materials needed for preservation purposes.

otherTitle.title: otherTitle
otherTitle.description: An additional or variant title, such as a title that was translated into English, a formal title that has been changed or corrected, an agency-supplied title, or an informal title. Variant titles include those that do not meet NARA standards for the formation of a title.
examples: Index to Series Described in Entry 36

pageNum.title: pageNum
pageNum.description: The page number of a digital object that is the target of a contribution, if applicable.

parentContributionId.title: parentContributionId
parentContributionId.description: Identifies the first version of a transcription or the comment being replied to.

partyDesignation.title: partyDesignation
partyDesignation.description: Specifies the an association of a archival record with a political party

personLinkCounts.title: personLinkCounts
personLinkCounts.description: Tracks the number and manner of the relationships to a person authority record.

contributor.description: Total number of times this person acts as a contributor to archival materials.

creator.description: Total number of times this person acts as a creator for archival materials.

donor.description: Total number of times this person acts as a donor of archival materials.

organization.description: Total number of times this object is referred to in an organization authority.

subject.description: Total number of times this person acts as the subject of archival materials.

totalDescription.description: Total number of times this person is referred to, in any way, in archival materials.

personName.title: personName
examples: Elizabeth, Queen, consort of George VI, King of Great Britain

personalContributor.title: personalContributor
personalContributor.description: The name of a person, other than the archival creator, responsible for the intellectual, technical, artistic, or financial production of the archival materials.

personalDonor.title: personalDonor
personalDonor.description: The name of a person who donated a collection of archival materials.

personalReference.title: personalReference
personalReference.description: The name of an individual who is the subject of or highly relevant to the archival materials.

personalTitle.title: personalTitle
personalTitle.description: Titles and other words associated with a person's name, including titles designating rank, office, or nobility; terms of address (Mr., Mrs.); initials for an academic degree (MBA, Dr), a roman numeral used with a surname; or other phrases associated with a name (Saint, Statesman).

copyStatus.description: The role or purpose of each physical occurrence (copy) of the archival materials. The purposes are preservation, reference, reproduction or some combination of the three.

extent.description: The measurement or amount of the archival materials themselves -- the linear measurement, page count, number of rolls, reels, cassettes, or number of data files.

physicalOccurrenceNote.description: Explanations or significant information relating to the physical occurrence of the archival materials.
examples: The cubic footage for the series of artifacts was estimated as one quarter of the stack area or 5,000 cubic feet.

predecessor.title: predecessor
predecessor.description: The name and identifier of the immediate predecessor organization.

productionDate.title: productionDate
productionDate.description: The date on which the item was first produced or created.

productionSeriesNumber.title: productionSeriesNumber
productionSeriesNumber.description: The alphabetic or numeric identifier for a part or section of a production series. Parts or sections refer to segments, such as individual episodes, in a production series. The word "series" is used in a non-archival sense.  It specifically refers to motion pictures or videotapes produced as a series, such as a television series or movie serials.

productionSeriesSubtitle.title: productionSeriesSubtitle
productionSeriesSubtitle.description: The title of a part or section of a production series.  Parts or sections refer to segments, such as individual episodes, in a production series. The word "series" is used in a non-archival sense. It specifically refers to motion pictures, sound recordings, or videotapes produced as a series, such as a television series or movie serials.

productionSeriesTitle.title: productionSeriesTitle
productionSeriesTitle.description: The name of a production series to which an audiovisual item belongs. The word "series" is used in a non-archival sense. It specifically refers to motion pictures, sound recordings, or videotapes produced as a series, such as a television series or movie serials.

programArea.title: programArea
programArea.description: The function or program area of the organization.

publicContributor.title: publicContributor
publicContributor.description: Information about the user who made the public contribution

publicContributorFullName.title: publicContributorFullName
publicContributorFullName.description: Display name of user who made the public contribution.

publicContributorHistory.title: publicContributorHistory
publicContributorHistory.description: Information about the user who made a public contribution that includes date and version information.

publicContributorNaraStaff.title: publicContributorNaraStaff
publicContributorNaraStaff.description: Identifies the contributions as being NARA Stff or not.

publicContributorUserId.title: publicContributorUserId
publicContributorUserId.description: Unique identifier of a user who made the public contribution.

qualifiableDate.title: qualifiableDate
qualifiableDate.description: A datetime with the means to qualify uncertainty.

day.description: Day of month in DD format (1-31)

logicalDate.description: Day, month, and year combined into a ISO 8601 date format: CCYY-MM-DD If day or month is unknown, they are replaced by 01-01 or 12-31 in this representation.

month.description: Month of year in MM format (1-12)

qualifier.description: The means for indicating an uncertain or approximate date.

recordGroupNumber.title: recordGroupNumber
recordGroupNumber.description: The unique number assigned to a record group.

recordSource.title: recordSource
recordSource.description: External authority source that provided the authority record.
examples: Library of Congress Name Authority File

recordType.title: recordType
recordType.description: Identifies the type of information stored on the record.

recordsCenterTransferNumber.title: recordsCenterTransferNumber
recordsCenterTransferNumber.description: The unique identifier assigned to a group of records that has been transferred into the physical custody of a records center. Formerly this was commonly known as the Records Center Accession Number

referenceUnit.title: referenceUnit
referenceUnit.description: Contact information of the unit that provides reference services for the archival materials.

address1.description: Street address of the point of contact / reference unit.

address2.description: Street address of the point of contact / reference unit.

city.description: City portion of address of the point of contact / reference unit.

email.description: Email address of the point of contact / reference unit.

fax.description: Fax number of the point of contact / reference unit

mailCode.description: Internal mailing code of the point of contact / reference unit.

name.description: Name of the point of contact / reference unit

phone.description: Phone number of the point of contact / reference unit.

postalCode.description: Postal code of the point of contact / reference unit.

state.description: State portion of address of the point of contact / reference unit.

relatedTerm.title: relatedTerm
relatedTerm.description: An authorized, preferred heading that is related in definition and scope but is not broader or narrower.

releaseDate.title: releaseDate
releaseDate.description: The date on which the audiovisual item was released for distribution.

role.title: role
role.description: Identifies roles that this person or organization fills.

contributor.description: Indicates if the person or organization acts as a contributor.

creator.description: Indicates if the person or organization acts as a contributor.

donor.description: Indicates if the person or organization acts as a donor

reference.description: Indicates if the person or organization is referenced by another person or org

roles.title: roles
roles.description: Identifies roles that this person or organization fills.

contributor.description: Indicates if the person or organization acts as a contributor.

creator.description: Indicates if the person or organization acts as a contributor.

donor.description: Indicates if the person or organization acts as a donor

subject.description: Indicates if the person or organization acts as a subject

scaleNote.title: scaleNote
scaleNote.description: The level of detail in cartographic or architectural documents expressed as a numerical ratio to one, which may have been computed from the verbal or bar scales included on the materials.
examples: The charts are drawn at the 1:250,000 scale (one inch equals 3.43 nautical miles).

scopeAndContentNote.title: scopeAndContentNote
scopeAndContentNote.description: The description of the breadth and depth of the record group, collection, or archival materials.
examples: This collection of donated historical materials consists of files maintained by Gerald M. Rafshoon's Atlanta-based advertising agency. The materials relate to the agency's work advising Jimmy Carter in his successful 1970 gubernatorial campaign, as well as his 1976 presidential campaign and his 1980 bid for re-election.

scopeNote.title: scopeNote
scopeNote.description: A note explaining the coverage, specialized usage, and/or rules for assigning the authority.
examples: Assign this heading to holdings limited to the indigenous Arctic peoples of Greenland, Canada, and northern Alaska.

Located at mouth of Hudson River; visited by Europeans 16th-17th cen.; settled by Dutch, seized by British in 1664; was cosmopolitan center & nations largest city by 18th cen. Today is huge metropolis composed of 5 boroughs corresponding to 5 counties.

seriesCount.title: seriesCount
seriesCount.description: Number of series within the archival control group.

seriesPhysicalOccurrence.title: seriesPhysicalOccurrence
seriesPhysicalOccurrence.description: Details about the physical occurrence of archival materials within a series.

containerList.description: The listing of the starting and ending titles of the contents of each container in which the archival materials are stored.
examples: Volume 1: A - D Volume 2: E - H

shortName.title: shortName
shortName.description: A version of the authorized name of an organization or person without additional information such as dates or fuller forms of names.
examples: Elizabeth, Queen, consort of George VI, King of Great Britain
Department of Defense. Department of the Navy. U.S. Naval Support Activity, Chu Lai, Vietnam.

shotList.title: shotList
shotList.description: A shot-by-shot description of a film or video.
examples: 1) GV Wave of LCMs underway, Corregidor under bombardment BG-SV.2) GV Paratrooper descends to Corregidor, many parachutes lay on ground.3) MS Plane passes paratroopers descend to Corregidor (good).4) MS Planes pass over island & drop paratroopers, paratroopers descend, many parachutes lay on island BG-SV.QUALITY: GENERALLY VERY GOOD.

soundType.title: soundType
soundType.description: The identification of the sound characteristics (absence or presence of sound) of audiovisual materials.

sourceNote.title: sourceNote
sourceNote.description: Resources cited to develop and support headings and references in an authority record.

specificAccessRestriction.title: specificAccessRestriction
specificAccessRestriction.description: Specific access restrictions to the archival materials, based on national security considerations, donor restrictions, court orders, and other statutory or regulatory provisions.

restriction.description: The specific reason for access restriction.
examples: Presidential Records Act (p)(1) National Security Classified

securityClassification.description: The highest level of national security protections or classified nuclear information protections on the archival materials.

specificRecordsTypeLinkCounts.title: specificRecordsTypeLinkCounts
specificRecordsTypeLinkCounts.description: Tracks the number and manner of the relationships to a specific records type authority record.

specificRecordsType.description: Total number of times this specific record type is referred to in another specifc records type authority record.

subject.description: Total number of times this specific record type acts as the subject of archival materials.

totalDescription.description: Total number of times this specific record type is referred to, in any way, in archival materials.

specificRecordsTypeReference.title: specificRecordsTypeReference
specificRecordsTypeReference.description: The intellectual format of the archival materials.

specificUseRestriction.title: specificUseRestriction
specificUseRestriction.description: The identification of the type of use restrictions, based on copyright, donor, or statutory provisions, on the archival materials.

subtitle.title: subtitle
subtitle.description: The secondary name in the formal title of the item, usually indicated by punctuation such as a colon or brackets.

successor.title: successor
successor.description: The name and identifier of the immediate successor ogranization.

title.title: title
title.description: The name assigned to the record group, collection, or archival materials.
examples: Records of the Office of the Pardon Attorney
Records of the War Labor Policies Board

topicalSubjectLinkCounts.title: topicalSubjectLinkCounts
topicalSubjectLinkCounts.description: Tracks the number and manner of the relationships to a topical subject authority record.

subject.description: Total number of times this topical subject acts as the subject of archival materials.

topicalSubject.description: Total number of times this topical subject is referred to in another topical subject.

totalDescription.description: Total number of times this topical subject is referred to, in any way, in archival materials.

topicalSubjectReference.title: topicalSubjectReference
topicalSubjectReference.description: The topics represented in the archival materials.

transferNote.title: transferNote
transferNote.description: Significant information regarding the internal transfer of archival materials from one NARA unit to another. This type of information is particularly important for archival materials that have been regionalized. Provides clarification needed to understand Internal Transfer Number or Records Center Transfer Number.
examples: These records were formerly housed with the textual records of the Polar Gift Collection in the former Division of Polar Archives and were transferred to the Still Picture Branch in January 1997.

updatedAt.title: updatedAt
updatedAt.description: Date and time the public contribution was updated.

useFor.title: useFor
useFor.description: The non-preferred, non-authorized form of a subject heading.

useRestriction.title: useRestriction
useRestriction.description: Provides information and status about restrictions on use of archival materials.

note.description: Significant information pertaining to the use or reproduction of the archival materials.
examples: Copyright held by RKO General Inc.
Some or all of the images in this series may be subject to copyright or other intellectual property restrictions.

status.description: Indication of whether or not there are use restrictions on the archival materials.

variantControlNumber.title: variantControlNumber
variantControlNumber.description: The control number or identifier given to the archival materials either by the creator or by NARA.  Allows users to retrieve the archival materials by any known control number.

note.description: The explanation of the variant control number and variant control type.
examples: Entry 194 is the entry number for this series found in Preliminary Inventory of the Cartographic Records of the American Expeditionary Forces, 1917-21 (Record Group 120) (PI 165)

number.description: The actual control number or identifier given to the archival materials.

type.description: The type of variant control number or identifier.

variantOrganizationName.title: variantOrganizationName
variantOrganizationName.description: Familiar or commonly used designations for an organization that varies from the organization's official name.

variantPersonName.title: variantPersonName
variantPersonName.description: Additional or variant names commonly used for the person, including nicknames, pen names, or transliterations.

collection.title: collection
collection.description: An artificial accumulation of documents brought together on the basis of some characteristic (e.g. means of acquisition, creator, subject, language, medium, form, name of collector) without regard to the provenance of the documents. The Presidential libraries often organize their archival materials by collections, which primarily fall into three categories: donated historical materials (relating to all Presidencies, Hoover-Bush), Presidential records (applying to Presidencies since Reagan), and Presidential historical materials (Nixon.)

comment.title: comment
comment.description: A form of public contribution that allows a user to share knowledge about the records, tell other users about related records, or share what someone might expect to find in a particular item, file unit, or series.

fileUnit.title: fileUnit
fileUnit.description: An organized unit (file, volume, etc.) of documents grouped together either for current use or in the process of archival arrangement. For NARA's descriptive practices, the file unit is the intellectual handling of the record item, which may or may not be the physical handling. In other words, a folder does not necessarily equal a file unit. For example, a case file may be in several physical folders, but is described as one file unit. For electronic records, the definition of a file unit level may be difficult. A file does not necessarily refer to a tape or to a particular data file.

ancestors.description: Identifiable and otherwise relevant information about archival control groups or hierarchical description levels that are parents or more distant ancestors to this file unit.

geographicPlaceName.title: geographicPlaceName
geographicPlaceName.description: Describes the places represented in archival materials.

item.title: item
item.description: The smallest indivisible archival unit (e.g. a letter, memorandum, report, leaflet, or photograph). For example, a book or record album would be described as an item, but the individual chapters of the book or the discs or songs that make up the album would not be described as items. Not used to describe motion picture films, sound recordings or video recordings.

itemAv.title: itemAv
itemAv.description: The smallest indivisible archival unit (e.g. a letter, memorandum, report, leaflet, or photograph). For example, a book or record album would be described as an item, but the individual chapters of the book or the discs or songs that make up the album would not be described as items.  Covers descriptions of audiovisual items such as motion picture films, sound recordings, and video recordings.

organization.title: organization
organization.description: Describes the purpose, history, function and relation to other organizations over time.

recordGroup.title: recordGroup
recordGroup.description: A body of organizationally related records established on the basis of provenance by an archives for control purposes; A major archival unit that comprises the records of a large organization, such as a Government bureau or independent agency.

series.title: series
series.description: File units or documents arranged in accordance with a filing system or maintained as a unit because they result from the same accumulation or filing process, the same function, or the same activity; have a particular form; or because of some other relationship arising out of their creation, receipt, or use.

ancestors.description: Identifiable and otherwise relevant information about archival control groups that are parents or more distant ancestors to this series.

formerAncestors.description: Identifiable and otherwise relevant information about archival control groups that at one time were parents or more distant ancestors to this description.

specificRecordsType.title: specificRecordsType
specificRecordsType.description: Describes the intellectual format of the archival materials.

tag.title: tag
tag.description: A form of user contribution that allows a user enter a word or phrase that describe and categorize the archival materials.

topicalSubject.title: TopicalSubject
topicalSubject.description: Describes the topics represented in archival materials.

transcription.title: transcription
transcription.description: A form of user contribution that allows a user to put into text the words contained within an archival record in order to increase searchability and make documents easier to read.

comment.example: a thoughtful edit to this comment

paramIngestionTimeStart.name: ingestTimeStart
paramIngestionTimeStart.description:
The ingestTimeStart parameter specifies the date or timestamp for fetching data.

Date only format: YYYY-MM-DD
    When provided in this format, the time defaults to 00:00:00.000000.
    Example: 2025-01-13 → interpreted as 2025-01-13 00:00:00.000000

 Full timestamp format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss.SSSSSS
     When provided in this format, data will be fetched for the exact timestamp specified.
     Example: 2025-01-13 14:30:15.123456 → data fetched at this precise datetime

Behavior:
   If both ingestTimeStart and ingestTimeEnd are provided, description records within that date/time range are retrieved.
   If only ingestTimeStart is provided, description records on and after that date/time are retrieved.
   If only ingestTimeEnd is provided, description records on and before that date/time are retrieved.

paramIngestionTimeEnd.name: ingestTimeEnd
paramIngestionTimeEnd.description:
The ingestTimeEnd parameter specifies the date or timestamp for fetching data.

Date only format: YYYY-MM-DD
    When provided in this format, the time defaults to 23:59:59.999999
    Example: 2025-01-13 → interpreted as 2025-01-13 23:59:59.999999

 Full timestamp format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss.SSSSSS
     When provided in this format, data will be fetched for the exact timestamp specified.
     Example: 2025-01-13 14:30:15.123456 → data fetched at this precise datetime

Behavior:
   If both ingestTimeStart and ingestTimeEnd are provided, description records within that date/time range are retrieved.
   If only ingestTimeStart is provided, description records on and after that date/time are retrieved.
   If only ingestTimeEnd is provided, description records on and before that date/time are retrieved.

paramControlNumbers.description:

paramStartDate.name: startDate
paramStartDate.description:
Date representing the start date of a range of dates. Must be in YYYY-MM-DD, YYYY-MM, or YYYY format and must be in the same format as endDate parameter, if one is provided.
Only ancestors with levelOfDescription as 'series' will have their date fields searched.

Searches the following date fields:
* record.productionDates.logicalDate
* record.copyrightDates.logicalDate
* record.releaseDates.logicalDate
* record.broadcastDates.logicalDate
* record.coverageStartDate.logicalDate
* record.coverageEndDate.logicalDate
* record.inclusiveStartDate.logicalDate
* record.inclusiveEndDate.logicalDate
* record.ancestors.inclusiveStartDate.logicalDate
* record.ancestors.inclusiveEndDate.logicalDate

paramEndDate.name: endDate
paramEndDate.description:
Date representing the end date of a range of dates. Must be in YYYY-MM-DD, YYYY-MM, or YYYY format and must be in the same format as startDate parameter, if one is provided.
Only ancestors with levelOfDescription as 'series' will have their date fields searched.

Searches the following date fields:
* record.productionDates.logicalDate
* record.copyrightDates.logicalDate
* record.releaseDates.logicalDate
* record.broadcastDates.logicalDate
* record.coverageStartDate.logicalDate
* record.coverageEndDate.logicalDate
* record.inclusiveStartDate.logicalDate

* record.ancestors.inclusiveStartDate.logicalDate
* record.ancestors.inclusiveEndDate.logicalDate

paramExactDate.name: exactDate

A single date. Must be in YYYY-MM-DD format.  Cannot be used with other date search parameters.  Used to search for records with dates that match exactly or that match the year and month but do not have a day
Only ancestors with levelOfDescription as 'series' will have their date fields searched.

Searches the following date fields:

If false, returns results where the field does not exist. Accepts nested fields at any level, for example `ancestors.inclusiveEndDate_exists=true`

paramIncludeExtractedText.name: includeExtractedText

paramIngestTimeStart.name: ingestTimeStart
paramIngestTimeStart.description:
The ingestTimeStart parameter specifies the date or timestamp for fetching data.

Date only format: YYYY-MM-DD
    When provided in this format, the time defaults to 00:00:00.000000.
    Example: 2025-01-13 → interpreted as 2025-01-13 00:00:00.000000

 Full timestamp format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss.SSSSSS
     When provided in this format, data will be fetched for the exact timestamp specified.
     Example: 2025-01-13 14:30:15.123456 → data fetched at this precise datetime

Behavior:
   If both ingestTimeStart and ingestTimeEnd are provided, description records within that date/time range are retrieved.
   If only ingestTimeStart is provided, description records on and after that date/time are retrieved.
   If only ingestTimeEnd is provided, description records on and before that date/time are retrieved.

paramIngestTimeEnd.name: ingestTimeEnd
paramIngestTimeEnd.description:
The ingestTimeEnd parameter specifies the date or timestamp for fetching data.

Date only format: YYYY-MM-DD
    When provided in this format, the time defaults to 23:59:59.999999
    Example: 2025-01-13 → interpreted as 2025-01-13 23:59:59.999999

 Full timestamp format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss.SSSSSS
     When provided in this format, data will be fetched for the exact timestamp specified.
     Example: 2025-01-13 14:30:15.123456 → data fetched at this precise datetime

Behavior:
   If both ingestTimeStart and ingestTimeEnd are provided, description records within that date/time range are retrieved.
   If only ingestTimeStart is provided, description records on and after that date/time are retrieved.
   If only ingestTimeEnd is provided, description records on and before that date/time are retrieved.

paramIncludeOtherExtractedText.name: includeOtherExtractedText
paramIncludeOtherExtractedText.description: Allows for the inclusion of other extracted text contributed by NARA partners in `record.digitalObjects.otherExtractedText` to be included in the response if any exists. Defaults to false.

paramIncludeTotalCount.name: includeTotalCount
paramIncludeTotalCount.description: Returns the total count of results in each result.

paramSearchAfterRecord.name: searchAfter
paramSearchAfterRecord.description:
Enables deep pagination to retrieve records beyond the first 10,000 results.  Not compatible with sort, or page parameters.

For the inital request use `searchAfter=`* and for all subsequent requests set `searchAfter` to the value of the sort array in the last member of `body.hits.hits` for each page.
